messages.content:
can I view the logs of this please
  
sudo systemctl status gtg-next.service

Running `systemctl status` will show you the current status and a short snippet of the logs of a service — so you'd see *whether it's active, failed, etc.*, plus the last few log lines from the journal.  

If you just want to view **the full logs**, use **`journalctl`**. For example:

```bash
# View recent logs from the specific service
sudo journalctl -u gtg-next.service

# Follow logs live, like `tail -f`
sudo journalctl -u gtg-next.service -f

# View logs since last boot
sudo journalctl -u gtg-next.service -b

# Limit to a certain time window
sudo journalctl -u gtg-next.service --since "1 hour ago"
```

If `systemctl status` doesn’t show enough information, switch to one of those commands.  

Would you like me to explain how to filter or save the logs to a file as well?
